_doc: Reviewer sign-off on coverage findings (docs/COVERAGE.md §'how the reviewer signs off on exceptions'). A finding matched here is downgraded from BLOCK to ACCEPTED by `tripod coverage`: it still appears in the ledger (tagged accepted, with the reason), but no longer fails the run. Governed: every entry carries provenance (accepted_by/on + sc_ref); this file is pinned in _spec/pins.json, so accepting an exception is a recorded, re-pinned edit — not a silent suppression. Match keys: UNMAPPED_SOURCE → (pericope, kind, gloss, verse-prefix); UNANCHORED_ENTITY → (pericope, kind, entity_id).

exceptions.note: 'Israel' (יִשְׂרָאֵל) occurs inside the divine title 'the God of Israel' (אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל) at 2:12. It qualifies the divine name, not a separately-tracked participant, so no PL_ISRAEL entity is warranted at this verse.
